perturbation.original_text: 4. “Confidential Information” means a Party’s energy management system model data provided or to be provided by a Disclosing Party to another Party. Confidential Information shall be disclosed only to Reviewing Representatives and only used to enable a Party to perform its Transmission Functions.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. “Confidential Information” means data shared between Parties. Confidential Information shall be disclosed to personnel.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition strictly limited disclosure of Confidential Information to 'Reviewing Representatives' and for 'Transmission Functions.' The revised definition broadens this significantly, allowing disclosure to 'personnel' (which is undefined and could include anyone within the organization) without specifying the information must be related to Transmission Functions. This contradicts Section B.2, which states Reviewing Representatives must execute a Non-Disclosure Certificate and be required to have the information for Transmission Functions responsibilities. The broadness of the term personnel in the changed definition creates uncertainty. Furthermore, the removed term 'energy management system model data' is not present, potentially leading to uncertainty on what is being reffered as confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Reviewing Representative. A Reviewing Representative shall not have access to any Confidential Information unless that Reviewing Representative is required to have the information in order to carry out that person’s Transmission Functions responsibilities and has executed the attached Non-Disclosure Certificate. The Reviewing Representative shall deliver a copy of his or her executed Non-Disclosure Certificate to PJM. A Reviewing Representative shall not use the Confidential Information or any portion thereof to give any Party or a competitor of any Party a competitive or commercial advantage. A Reviewing Representative may make copies or Notes of Confidential Information that shall be subject to this Agreement. In the event a Reviewing Representative ceases to be employed or engaged by a Party, or is employed, retained, or given duties that include Competitive Duties, (i) the Reviewing Representative shall continue to comply with the terms and conditions of this Agreement with respect to the Confidential Information to which such person previously had access, (ii) the Party shall terminate the Reviewing Representative's access to Confidential Information, and (iii) the Party shall cause the Reviewing Representative to return or dispose of the Confidential Information, or transfer the information to another Reviewing Representative of the Party. Each Party shall advise the Reviewing Representatives that Confidential Information is confidential and shall be treated as confidential in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the scope of who can access 'Confidential Information' from 'Reviewing Representatives' to 'personnel' and removes the 'Transmission Functions' limitation. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which specifically limits access to Reviewing Representatives who need the information for Transmission Functions and have signed a Non-Disclosure Certificate. The changed definition makes the requirements in the contradicted text unenforceable, as anyone within the organization could potentially have access without meeting the defined criteria, leading to a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 7. “Non-Disclosure Certificate” means the certificate annexed hereto by which Reviewing Representatives seeking access to Confidential Information shall certify their understanding that such access to Confidential Information is provided pursuant to the terms and conditions of this Agreement and that each Reviewing Representative has read this Agreement and agrees to be bound by it.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause that the Non-Disclosure Certificate is for Reviewing Representatives seeking access to Confidential Information and certifies their understanding and agreement to be bound by the agreement terms, the definition no longer connects the certificate to the core purpose of confidentiality or the individuals handling sensitive data. This creates an in-text contradiction with section B.2 because B.2 still mentions Non-Disclosure Certificates, but there is no longer a clear definition clarifying its purpose or how it enforces confidentiality. This omission creates ambiguity and weakens the enforcement of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Reviewing Representative. A Reviewing Representative shall not have access to any Confidential Information unless that Reviewing Representative is required to have the information in order to carry out that person’s Transmission Functions responsibilities and has executed the attached Non-Disclosure Certificate. The Reviewing Representative shall deliver a copy of his or her executed Non-Disclosure Certificate to PJM. A Reviewing Representative shall not use the Confidential Information or any portion thereof to give any Party or a competitor of any Party a competitive or commercial advantage. A Reviewing Representative may make copies or Notes of Confidential Information that shall be subject to this Agreement. In the event a Reviewing Representative ceases to be employed or engaged by a Party, or is employed, retained, or given duties that include Competitive Duties, (i) the Reviewing Representative shall continue to comply with the terms and conditions of this Agreement with respect to the Confidential Information to which such person previously had access, (ii) the Party shall terminate the Reviewing Representative's access to Confidential Information, and (iii) the Party shall cause the Reviewing Representative to return or dispose of the Confidential Information, or transfer the information to another Reviewing Representative of the Party. Each Party shall advise the Reviewing Representatives that Confidential Information is confidential and shall be treated as confidential in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines "Non-Disclosure Certificate" as a certificate ensuring Reviewing Representatives understand and agree to the agreement's terms. The changed text reduces this to simply "a form." This omission creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text (Section B.2), which requires the certificate's execution as a prerequisite for accessing confidential information. By stripping the certificate of its defined purpose relating to confidentiality agreements, the changed text contradicts the requirement in Section B.2 that the certificate ensures understanding and compliance, making the agreement's enforcement unclear and inconsistent.

perturbation.original_text: 9. “Reviewing Representative” means an employee of a Party who has signed or electronically signed a Non-Disclosure Certificate and who is a principal, partner, officer, director, employee, agent and other representative of a Party. Reviewing Representative may include a person whose duties include Competitive Duties so long as such Reviewing Representative’s receipt and use of Confidential Information is not prohibited conduct under FERC’s rules, including Standards of Conduct.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. “Reviewing Representative” means a person of a Party.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition specifies the Reviewing Representative has to sign a Non-Disclosure Certificate. By removing this requirement and the reference to FERC's rules/Standards of Conduct, the definition is significantly broadened. This contradicts section B.4, which states Parties and Reviewing Representatives who have executed this Agreement or a Non-Disclosure Certificate may discuss Confidential Information. The modified definition, coupled with the omission, raises uncertainty on who is a Reviewing Representative, and the rules governing the process regarding disclosure of Confidential Information
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Discussions of Confidential Information. Parties and Reviewing Representatives who have executed this Agreement or a Non-Disclosure Certificate may discuss Confidential Information with other Parties and Reviewing Representatives who have executed this Agreement or a Non-Disclosure Certificate.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Reviewing Representative' as someone who has signed a Non-Disclosure Certificate and potentially performs Competitive Duties under FERC rules. The changed text omits the Non-Disclosure Certificate requirement and the reference to FERC rules, broadening the definition. This directly contradicts section 4, which states that only Parties and Reviewing Representatives who have executed the Agreement or a Non-Disclosure Certificate may discuss Confidential Information. The change creates uncertainty as to who is a Reviewing Representative and who is allowed to discuss Confidential Information, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3. List of Reviewing Representatives. PJM shall receive and maintain copies of all Non-Disclosure Certificates executed by the Party’s Reviewing Representatives, and shall maintain a list of the Reviewing Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. List of Personnel. PJM shall maintain a list of personnel.
perturbation.explanation: The original text obligates PJM to maintain a list of 'Reviewing Representatives' and copies of their Non-Disclosure Certificates. This is directly linked to ensuring only authorized individuals have access to confidential information. The modified text removes the 'Non-Disclosure Certificates' requirement and changes the maintained list to 'personnel'. Given that the Reviewing Representatives have been changed to Person of a party it is contradictory to the original terms set of protecting confidetial information. This removal creates uncertainty on who is accountable. This contradicts the intentions stated in the agreement's recitals regarding protecting confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RECITALS: WHEREAS , PJM serves as the Regional Transmission Organization with reliability and/or functional control responsibilities over transmission systems involving all or parts of thirteen states and the District of Columbia, and operates and oversees wholesale markets for electricity pursuant to the requirements of the PJM Open Access Transmission Tariff (“PJM Tariff”) and the Amended and Restated Operating Agreement of PJM Interconnection, L.L.C. (“Operating Agreement”); and WHEREAS, the Transmission Owner s recognize that, while PJM serves as the Regiona l Transmission Organization in the PJM region, the Transmission Owners within the PJM region perform certain Transmission Functions, as Transmission Functions is defined in section 18 C.F.R. § 358 of the FERC rules and regulations, with respect to their individual transmission systems and distribution systems. WHEREAS , the Parties desire to enter into this Agreement to protect and maintain from disclosure to third parties the Confidential Information that will be exchanged to facilitate reliable operations; and WHEREAS, this Agreement is a statement of the conditions and requirements, consistent with the requirements of the Operating Agreement, whereby a Disclosing Party may provide Confidential Information to a Party for the purpose of the Party executing its Transmission Functions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for PJM to maintain copies of Non-Disclosure Certificates and changes 'Reviewing Representatives' to 'personnel'. This contradicts the RECITALS section, which states the Parties desire to protect Confidential Information. By removing the requirement for Non-Disclosure Certificates, the changed text weakens the measures to protect confidential information, conflicting with the stated purpose of the agreement. This directly breaks the compatibility between the new requirement and the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Ownership and Use of Confidential Information. All Confidential Information delivered by a Disclosing Party to a Party pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the Disclosing Party, and such Confidential Information shall be promptly returned to the Disclosing Party upon request. That portion of the Confidential Information that may be found in analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by or for a Party and all Confidential Information that is oral will be kept by a Party subject to the terms of this Agreement or destroyed. Neither the Party nor its Reviewing Representatives shall use the Confidential Information for any purpose whatsoever except for the purpose of executing the Reviewing Party’s Transmission Functions. Once the Party no longer requires the use of such information for the purpose of performing its Transmission Functions, the Confidential Information shall be returned or destroyed in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Ownership and Use of Information. All Information delivered by a Disclosing Party to a Party pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of the Disclosing Party, and such Information shall be promptly returned to the Disclosing Party upon request. That portion of the Information that may be found in analyses, compilations, studies or other documents prepared by or for a Party and all Information that is oral will be kept by a Party subject to the terms of this Agreement or destroyed. Neither the Party nor its personnel shall use the Information for any purpose whatsoever. Once the Party no longer requires the use of such information, the Information shall be returned or destroyed in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Information' throughout the section and replacing 'Reviewing Representatives' with 'personnel', the scope of this clause is significantly altered. The key term 'Transmission Functions' is also removed. The removal creates in-text contradictions since section A4 refers to 'Confidential Information'. The removal of 'Transmission Functions' creates uncertainty and weakens the protection of energy management system model data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. “Confidential Information” means data shared between Parties. Confidential Information shall be disclosed to personnel.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Information' broadens the scope of the clause in Section 8, while Section A4 specifically defines 'Confidential Information'. This creates a contradiction because Section 8 now applies to all 'Information', while Section A4 refers only to 'Confidential Information', leading to uncertainty about which data is covered by each section. The removal of 'Transmission Functions' further exacerbates the issue as the original text limited the use of 'Confidential Information' to 'Transmission Functions', providing a clear boundary. By removing 'Transmission Functions', the changed text removes this clear boundary causing further uncertainty and contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Identification of Confidential Information . Confidential Information that is in writing or other tangible form (including electronic form) shall be subject to this Agreement only if it is clearly marked as "Confidential" when disclosed by the Disclosing Party to a Party. Confidential Information that is provided orally shall be subject to this Agreement only if its confidential nature is announced at the time of disclosure and an outline of the scope of the information provided is reduced to writing, with a copy provided to the Party within ten (10) calendar days of oral disclosure of the information. Inadvertent failure to mark Confidential Information as “Confidential” at the time it is disclosed shall not be deemed a waiver by the Disclosing Party of the protections of this Agreement provided that such Confidential Information is identified and marked "Confidential" promptly upon the discovery of its inadvertent disclosure. Confidential Information excludes any information that: (i) the Disclosing Party has not specifically notified the Party is confidential; (ii) becomes available to the Party or the Reviewing Representative on a non-confidential basis from a source other than: (a) the Disclosing Party, or other person acting on behalf of the Disclosing Party; or (b) a Party who has confidentiality obligations to the Disclosing Party; (iii) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by the Party or its Reviewing Representatives; (iv) was previously known to the Party or its Reviewing Representative free and clear of any obligation to keep it confidential; (v) is disclosed to third parties by the Disclosing Party without restriction or obligation of confidentiality; (vi) is developed independently by the Party as evidenced by documentation made in the ordinary course of business by the Party; or (vii) the Disclosing Party notifies the Party that such information is no longer Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Identification of Information. Information that is in writing or other tangible form (including electronic form) shall be subject to this Agreement only if it is marked. Inadvertent failure to mark Information at the time it is disclosed shall not be deemed a waiver by the Disclosing Party of the protections of this Agreement provided that such Information is identified and marked promptly upon the discovery of its inadvertent disclosure. Information excludes any information that: (i) the Disclosing Party has not specifically notified the Party is confidential; (ii) becomes available to the Party on a non-confidential basis from a source other than: (a) the Disclosing Party, or other person acting on behalf of the Disclosing Party; or (b) a Party who has confidentiality obligations to the Disclosing Party; (iii) is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of a disclosure by the Party; (iv) was previously known to the Party free and clear of any obligation to keep it confidential; (v) is disclosed to third parties by the Disclosing Party without restriction or obligation of confidentiality; (vi) is developed independently by the Party as evidenced by documentation made in the ordinary course of business by the Party; or (vii) the Disclosing Party notifies the Party that such information is no longer Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The term "Confidential" is dropped from the requirement of marking the Information in order to follow this agreement and the term Reviewing Representative has been removed when determining if information is to be excluded. The term Confidential Information is still being reffered in the section which creates a contradiction. The removal of the requirements for 'Reviewing Representative' causes a contradiction because this part of the clause excludes Information that the 'Reviewing Representative' became aware of but there is no more definition. It creates ambiguity and reduces enforceability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. “Reviewing Representative” means a person of a Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'Confidential' from 'Confidential Information', and removes 'Reviewing Representative'. The definition of Reviewing Representative is still used in the document but no longer defined, which creates a contradiction with the original definition. The modified text refers to 'Information' instead of 'Confidential Information', creating an inconsistency. While the exclusion criteria still reference the Disclosing Party notifying the Party that information is no longer 'Confidential Information', the definition of Information doesn't specify it must be confidential. This directly contradicts the intention of the Contradicted Text, as now a generic term is being used where it should be specifically referring to Confidential information. The change introduces ambiguity where the contradicted clause expects certainty.
